summary: A fortified medieval-style structure featuring a central tall tower with stepped/crenellated roof, flanked by two smaller towers with similar architectural details. The building has a rectangular footprint with defensive walls, stone block construction, and decorative gold/brass accents. Multiple viewing angles reveal a complex multi-tower design with internal courtyard spaces and strategic tower placement.

shape.overall_form: rectangular compound with three prominent towers (one central, two flanking)
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry with central dominant tower

rebuild_hints: Build central tower first as tallest structure (approximately 14 blocks high)
Construct two flanking towers on left and right sides, slightly shorter than center
Use stonebrick for primary walls with brick accents for visual detail
Add gold blocks as decorative trim on tower tops and wall edges
Create crenellated/stepped roof design using slabs on all three towers
Enclose structure with defensive walls connecting the towers
Include internal courtyard space visible from top-down view
Place dark wood flooring in interior chambers
Ensure bilateral symmetry for flanking towers
Add stone block foundation/base layer around perimeter
uncertainties: Exact internal room layout and floor divisions unclear from exterior views
Precise height of each tower relative to others uncertain; estimate based on visual proportion
Material composition of interior floors not fully visible; dark wood inferred from limited views
Exact crenellation pattern and slab arrangement requires closer inspection
Depth dimension estimated from perspective; could vary by 2-3 blocks
Presence of windows or openings on tower sides not clearly visible in all angles
